title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Genes can spliced different ways — certain exons may included transcripts skipped others. called alternative splicing, means one gene can produce multiple protein variants different functions. Matisse measures alternative splicing one cell time. cell calculates PSI value (Percent Spliced ) splicing event: PSI = 1 — every transcript cell includes exon PSI = 0 — every transcript skips exon PSI = 0.5 — half include, half skip comparing PSI values across cell types, clusters, conditions can find populations splice genes differently — much. Matisse sits top existing Seurat workflow. gene expression data, UMAP, cluster labels stay intact; splicing information added alongside .

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: Matisse supports two data types require slightly different starting steps: object built, downstream functions — QC, filtering, normalisation, visualisation — identical data types.

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: → Full walkthrough: Short-read workflow
code: library(Matisse)  obj <- CreateMatisseObject(   seurat          = seu,          # your existing Seurat object   junction_counts = jxn_counts,   # cells × junctions from STARsolo   event_data      = event_df      # splice event annotation ) obj <- CalculatePSI(obj, min_coverage = 5) obj <- ComputeIsoformQC(obj) PlotPSIUMAP(obj, event_id = "PTBP1:SE:chr18:3433647-3436055")

title: Getting started with Matisse
text: → Full walkthrough: Long-read workflow
code: library(Matisse)  obj <- CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts(   seurat            = seu,   transcript_counts = tx_counts,    # transcripts × cells from Bagpiper/FLAMES   ioe_files         = "events_SE.ioe",   min_coverage      = 5L ) # PSI is already computed; normalise transcripts then cluster obj <- SCTransformTranscripts(obj, n_pca_dims = 50) obj <- RunUMAP(obj, dims = 1:50) obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, dims = 1:50) obj <- FindClusters(obj, resolution = 0.5) PlotPSIUMAP(obj, event_id = "SE:chr18:3433647-3436055:+")

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: Use workflow data generated long-read platform (PacBio, Oxford Nanopore) used short-read isoform quantifier Bagpiper, FLAMES, LIQA produces per-transcript per-cell counts. tools assign read specific full-length transcript rather counting junction level. STARsolo junction counts standard 10x Chromium short reads, see Short-read workflow instead.

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: PSI calculated automatically construction — separate CalculatePSI() call needed. cell × event entry fraction transcripts carrying included form exon: PSIc,e=∑inclusion transcript counts∑inclusion counts+∑exclusion countsPSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion transcript counts}}                    {\sum \text{inclusion counts} + \sum \text{exclusion counts}} Cells fewer min_coverage total transcript counts given event left NA.
code: library(Matisse)  # transcript_counts: transcripts × cells sparse matrix (e.g. from Bagpiper) # ioe_files: SUPPA2 .ioe output files obj <- CreateMatisseObjectFromTranscripts(   seurat            = seu,   transcript_counts = transcript_counts,   ioe_files         = c(     "events_SE.ioe",   # skipped exons     "events_RI.ioe",   # retained introns     "events_SS.ioe"    # alternative splice sites   ),   min_coverage = 5L )

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: Check cell enough transcript coverage moving clustering. Remove low-coverage cells uninformative events:
code: obj <- ComputeIsoformQC(obj) PlotQCMetrics(obj, group_by = "cell_type") obj <- FilterCells(obj, min_pct_covered = 10) obj <- FilterEvents(obj, min_cells_covered = 20, min_psi_variance = 0.01)

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: long-read data transcript-level count matrix often high technical variability. SCTransformTranscripts() runs SCTransform "transcript" assay PCA components default — useful isoform variation subtler gene-expression variation benefits wider PCA space.
code: # Normalise, regress out sequencing depth, and run PCA in one step. # Increase n_pca_dims if clusters look under-resolved. obj <- SCTransformTranscripts(obj, n_pca_dims = 50)

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: Standard Seurat clustering functions work directly Matisse object. PSI data slots preserved throughout.
code: obj <- RunUMAP(obj, dims = 1:50) obj <- FindNeighbors(obj, dims = 1:50) obj <- FindClusters(obj, resolution = 0.5)

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: dot cell coloured PSI value one event: blue = exon skipped (low PSI), red = exon included (high PSI).
code: PlotPSIUMAP(   obj,   event_id = "SE:chr18:3433647-3436055:+",   title    = "PTBP1 exon 9 — PSI per cell" )

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
code: PlotPSIViolin(   obj,   event_id = "SE:chr18:3433647-3436055:+",   group_by = "cell_type" )

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
text: Note event IDs: SUPPA2 event IDs use format TYPE:chr:coords:strand (e.g. SE:chr18:100-200:300-400:+). become column names GetPSI(obj), use string calling PlotPSIUMAP() subsetting obj[, event_id].
code: PlotPSIHeatmap(obj, group_by = "seurat_clusters", max_cells = 400)

title: Long-read workflow: transcript-based PSI
code: # Extract the underlying Seurat object seu <- GetSeurat(obj)  # Cell metadata via $ (forwarded from Seurat) obj$seurat_clusters obj$cell_type  # Full PSI matrix as a sparse matrix (cells × events) psi <- GetPSI(obj)  # Raw transcript counts (transcripts × cells) tx <- GetTranscriptCounts(obj)  # Subset to one cell type — PSI and gene expression stay in sync neurons <- obj[obj$cell_type == "Neuron", ]

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
text: Use workflow data comes standard 10x Chromium short reads processed STARsolo STAR. tools can output per-cell junction count table alongside gene expression matrix. Matisse uses junction counts compute PSI (Percent Spliced ) splicing event cell. full-transcript counts long-read platform transcript quantifier (Bagpiper, FLAMES, LIQA), see Long-read workflow instead.

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
text: don’t hand-curated event annotation, BuildSimpleEvents() can generate one automatically junction table — junction becomes “simple” event junctions gene treated exclusion evidence:
code: library(Matisse)  obj <- CreateMatisseObject(   seurat          = seu,   junction_counts = jxn_counts,   # cells × junctions matrix from STARsolo   event_data      = event_df,     # splice event annotation table   junction_data   = junction_df   # optional: genomic coordinates per junction ) event_df <- BuildSimpleEvents(junction_df)

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
text: cell event, Matisse sums reads inclusion-supporting junctions exclusion-supporting junctions, computes: PSIc,e=∑inclusion junction reads∑inclusion reads+∑exclusion readsPSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion junction reads}}                    {\sum \text{inclusion reads} + \sum \text{exclusion reads}} Cells fewer min_coverage total reads event left NA.
code: obj <- CalculatePSI(obj, min_coverage = 5)

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
text: Remove cells sparse events covered cells:
code: obj <- ComputeIsoformQC(obj) PlotQCMetrics(obj, group_by = "cell_type") obj <- FilterCells(   obj,   min_junctions      = 5,   min_junction_reads = 20,   min_pct_covered    = 10 )  obj <- FilterEvents(   obj,   min_cells_covered = 20,   min_psi_variance  = 0.01 )

code: PlotJunctionCoverage(obj, gene_id = "PTBP1", group_by = "cell_type")

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
code: # Extract the Seurat object for any standard Seurat step seu <- GetSeurat(obj)  # Cell metadata (forwarded from the embedded Seurat object) obj$seurat_clusters obj$cell_type  # PSI matrix: cells × events psi <- GetPSI(obj)  # Raw junction counts: cells × junctions jxn <- GetJunctionCounts(obj)  # Subset to a specific cell type neurons <- obj[obj$cell_type == "Neuron", ]

title: Short-read workflow: junction-based PSI
text: multiple samples want analyse together, merge individual Matisse objects running QC:
code: # Process each sample independently up to CreateMatisseObject + CalculatePSI, # then merge combined <- MergeMatisse(   obj_sample1, obj_sample2,   add_cell_ids = c("S1", "S2") )

what: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: Computes Percent Spliced (PSI) matrix splice events defined event_data. applies objects junction mode; event mode PSI computed construction time.

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
code: CalculatePSI(object, events = NULL, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' CalculatePSI(   object,   events = NULL,   min_coverage = 5L,   na_fill = NA_real_,   verbose = TRUE )  # S4 method for class 'ANY' CalculatePSI(   object,   events,   min_coverage = 5L,   na_fill = NA_real_,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: object MatisseObject junction mode, sparse matrix (cells × junctions). events object matrix: data.frame columns event_id, inclusion_junctions, exclusion_junctions. object MatisseObject defaults GetEventData(object). ... Additional arguments passed method. min_coverage Integer. Minimum total reads per cell per event report PSI value. Default: 5. na_fill Numeric. Replacement low-coverage entries. Default: NA_real_. verbose Logical. Print progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: MatisseObject (given one) PSI matrix. MatisseObject: input object "psi" assay populated inside embedded Seurat object. matrix: dense matrix (cells × events) PSI values.

title: Calculate PSI matrix from junction counts — CalculatePSI
text: cell \(c\) event \(e\): $$PSI_{c,e} = \frac{\sum \text{inclusion reads}}                        {\sum \text{inclusion reads} +                         \sum \text{exclusion reads}}$$ Results stored inside embedded Seurat object Assay5 named "psi", : "data" layer: PSI values \([0,1]\) (events × cells). "counts" layer: inclusion read counts (events × cells). "exclusion" layer: exclusion read counts (events × cells). Entries total coverage falls min_coverage set NA "data" layer.

what: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: Calculates panel QC metrics junction count PSI layers stores directly embedded Seurat object's meta.data. Existing columns names overwritten.

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
code: ComputeIsoformQC(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' ComputeIsoformQC(object, min_coverage = 5L, verbose = TRUE)

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: object MatisseObject. ... Additional arguments (unused). min_coverage Integer. Minimum total reads consider event "covered" computing n_events_covered. Default: 5. verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: updated MatisseObject QC columns added cell metadata. MatisseObject QC columns added MatisseMeta(object) (.e. Seurat meta.data).

title: Compute per-cell isoform QC metrics — ComputeIsoformQC
text: Computed metrics: n_junctions_detected Number junctions least one read (junction mode ). total_junction_reads Total junction read count across junctions (junction mode ). n_events_covered Number splice events PSI \(\geq\) min_coverage (requires PSI calculated first). pct_events_covered Percentage events sufficient coverage. mean_psi Mean PSI across covered events.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: single constructor MatisseObject. Combines Seurat object isoform-resolved splicing data. operating mode detected automatically inputs supply:

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
code: CreateMatisseObject(   seurat,   junction_counts = NULL,   transcript_counts = NULL,   ioe_files = NULL,   event_data = NULL,   junction_data = NULL,   min_coverage = 5L,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: seurat Seurat object. Required. junction_counts sparse matrix (dgCMatrix, cells × junctions) raw per-junction read counts. Row names must match colnames(seurat). Triggers junction mode. Default: NULL. transcript_counts matrix sparse matrix (transcripts × cells) raw transcript-level counts. Stored Assay5("transcript") Seurat object. Column names must overlap colnames(seurat). Default: NULL. ioe_files Character vector paths SUPPA2 .ioe files. supplied together transcript_counts, triggers event mode PSI computed construction. Default: NULL. event_data data.frame defining splice events (junction mode ). Required columns: event_id, gene_id, chr, strand, event_type, inclusion_junctions, exclusion_junctions. Default: NULL. junction_data data.frame junction annotations. Required columns: junction_id, chr, start, end, strand, gene_id. Default: NULL. min_coverage Integer. Minimum total transcript counts per cell per event report PSI value (event mode ). Default: 5. verbose Logical. Print construction progress. Default: TRUE.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: MatisseObject.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
text: Junction mode (short-read): pass junction_counts. Junction counts stored Assay5("junction") inside Seurat object. Call CalculatePSI afterwards compute PSI values. Event mode (long-read): pass transcript_counts ioe_files. Transcript counts stored Assay5("transcript") PSI computed immediately supplied SUPPA2 .ioe event definitions, stored Assay5("psi"). can also pass transcript_counts alone (without ioe_files) store transcript assay without computing PSI — example, want run SCTransform transcript-level counts compute PSI separately.

title: Create a MatisseObject — CreateMatisseObject
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(Seurat) counts <- matrix(rpois(200, 5), nrow = 20,                  dimnames = list(paste0("Gene", 1:20),                                  paste0("Cell", 1:10))) seu <- CreateSeuratObject(counts)  # Junction mode jxn <- make_junction_counts() obj <- CreateMatisseObject(seurat = seu, junction_counts = jxn)  # Event mode tx  <- make_transcript_counts() obj <- CreateMatisseObject(seurat = seu, transcript_counts = tx,                            ioe_files = "path/to/events.ioe") } # }

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
code: FilterCells(object, ...)  # S4 method for class 'MatisseObject' FilterCells(   object,   min_junctions = NULL,   max_junctions = NULL,   min_junction_reads = NULL,   max_junction_reads = NULL,   min_pct_covered = NULL,   custom_filters = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells
text: object MatisseObject. ... Named numeric thresholds; see FilterCells. min_junctions Integer. Minimum n_junctions_detected. Default: NULL (filter). max_junctions Integer. Maximum n_junctions_detected. Default: NULL. min_junction_reads Integer. Minimum total_junction_reads. Default: NULL. max_junction_reads Integer. Maximum total_junction_reads. Default: NULL. min_pct_covered Numeric (0–100). Minimum pct_events_covered. Default: NULL. custom_filters Named list two-element numeric vectors c(min, max) applied arbitrary metadata columns. Use NA one-sided bound. Default: NULL. verbose Logical. Default: TRUE.

title: Filter cells by isoform QC thresholds — FilterCells

what: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Matisse provides integrated framework isoform-resolved single-cell RNA-seq analysis, built top Seurat Signac. Key capabilities: MatisseObject — S4 class wraps Seurat object co-stores junction counts, PSI matrices, splice event annotations, keeping gene expression isoform layers synchronised. PSI calculation — CalculatePSI computes per-cell Percent Spliced values raw junction read counts user-supplied auto-generated event annotation table. Isoform QC — ComputeIsoformQC derives per-cell metrics (junction detection rate, event coverage, mean PSI); FilterCells FilterEvents enforce quality thresholds. Visualization — UMAP overlays, violin plots, PSI heatmaps, junction coverage bar charts via consistent ggplot2-based API.

title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Full documentation vignettes available https://avisrilab.github.io/Matisse.

title: Matisse: Multi-modal Analysis of Transcript Isoforms in Single-Cell Sequencing Experiments — Matisse-package
text: Maintainer: k3yavi avisrilab@gmail.com

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: central data structure Matisse. wraps Seurat object adds isoform-resolved splicing layers. per-cell data — junction counts, PSI values, transcript counts, QC metrics — live inside embedded Seurat object named assays (Assay5) cell metadata (meta.data). Nothing duplicated outside Seurat object.

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: Two operating modes supported, set automatically construction: "junction" Short-read mode. Raw junction counts stored Assay5("junction") (junctions × cells). PSI computed later CalculatePSI stored Assay5("psi"). "event" Long-read mode. Transcript counts (e.g. Bagpiper FLAMES) stored Assay5("transcript"). PSI computed construction time SUPPA2 .ioe event definitions stored Assay5("psi").

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: show(MatisseObject): Display summary MatisseObject. x[[: Access cell metadata Seurat slots via [[. Checks seurat@meta.data first; falls back embedded Seurat object (assays, reductions, etc.).

title: The MatisseObject S4 class — MatisseObject-class
text: seurat Seurat object. Contains per-cell data: gene expression, splice assays ("junction", "transcript", "psi"), cell metadata (QC metrics, cluster labels), dimensionality reductions. event_data data.frame one row per splice event. Required columns: event_id, gene_id, chr, strand, event_type, inclusion_junctions, exclusion_junctions. junction_data data.frame one row per junction. Required columns: junction_id, chr, start, end, strand, gene_id. mode Character. "junction" short-read objects; "event" long-read objects. version Character string recording Matisse version used create object. misc Named list user-defined extra data.

title: Matisse 0.1.0
text: New MatisseObject S4 class wrapping Seurat object isoform-resolved layers: junction counts, PSI matrix, inclusion/exclusion counts, event junction annotation tables, per-cell isoform metadata. CreateMatisseObject(): primary constructor input validation automatic cell-barcode alignment. CalculatePSI(): computes PSI matrices junction count data splice event definitions. Supports MatisseObject bare matrix input. SummarizePSI(): per-event summary statistics (mean, median, sd, coverage). ComputeIsoformQC(): per-cell QC metrics including junction detection rate, total junction reads, event coverage, mean PSI. FilterCells() FilterEvents(): threshold-based filtering informative removal summaries. Visualization: PlotPSIUMAP(), PlotPSIViolin(), PlotPSIHeatmap(), PlotJunctionCoverage(), PlotQCMetrics(). BuildSimpleEvents(): convenience helper generating one-vs-rest event tables junction annotation. MergeMatisse(): concatenate two MatisseObjects cell-prefix deduplication. GitHub Actions workflows R CMD check (multi-OS), pkgdown website deployment, test coverage reporting.
